howItWorks.intro: This valentine's day spending calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Spend = Dinner + Gifts + Flowers + Activities
National averages based on NRF Valentine's Day spending surveys
Tip & Tax estimate = Dinner Budget x 30% (20% tip + 10% tax)
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How much does the average person spend on Valentine's Day?
faq.answer: The average American spends about $185 on Valentine's Day according to NRF data, though this varies widely. Spending typically peaks during the serious/engaged stage and decreases for longer-term couples.

faq.question: Is it worth spending a lot on Valentine's Day?
faq.answer: Research shows experiences (dinners, trips) create more lasting happiness than material gifts. A thoughtful $30 experience often means more than an expensive but impersonal gift.

faq.question: How can I save on Valentine's Day?
faq.answer: Celebrate the weekend before or after (restaurant prices drop), cook at home, buy flowers from a grocery store instead of a florist, and focus on handmade or experience-based gifts.

sections.title: What Is the Valentine's Day Spending Calculator?
sections.body: The Valentine's Day Spending Calculator helps you plan your spending with confidence so you stay on budget without cutting corners. Instead of guessing or spending hours on manual calculations, get accurate results in seconds. Enter your details above and let the calculator do the work.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Budgeting decisions are easier when you can see the complete picture before committing to spending. This calculator breaks down the real costs so you can allocate your money with confidence and avoid overspending in areas that matter less to you.
